label: Mid-1970s America
description: Post-Vietnam, oil crisis era. Suburban sprawl at peak. American cars dominated. Brands were bold and simple.

specific_models: 1973-1977 Chevrolet Caprice Classic — full-size, chrome bumpers, vinyl roof
1973-1979 Ford LTD — full-size sedan, wide body, bench seat
1974-1978 Dodge Monaco — large chrome grille, two-tone paint
1971-1976 Buick LeSabre — pillarless hardtop, whitewall tires
1971-1976 Chevrolet Impala — full-size, chrome rear bumper
1971-1976 Plymouth Fury — full-size, vinyl roof common
1973-1977 Pontiac Catalina — full-size, chrome trim heavy
1975-1979 Chevrolet Monza — small hatchback, rare but present
1973-1979 Ford F-100 — square body, chrome grille
1973-1980 Chevrolet C10 — square body, chrome bumper
1972-1980 Dodge D100 — angular cab, simple grille

fuel.context: Post-1973 oil embargo, prices had risen sharply from $0.36 in 1972

characteristics: parking lots directly in front of stores
mechanical price signs with changeable numbers
mechanical price boards at gas stations
signage.typography_style: bold sans-serif, slab serif, occasional script

characteristics: green highway signs with white lettering
older style stop signs smaller than modern

items: metal trash cans with dome lids
wooden utility poles with glass insulators

advertising.style: bold graphic, hand-illustrated elements, limited photography in signage

lighting.street_lights: mercury vapor or early sodium vapor — bluish-white or orange glow
lighting.commercial: fluorescent interior lighting visible through storefronts

downtown_street.narrative: A working small-town main street in 1975 — locally owned shops with hand-painted and neon signs, a five & dime, and not a single modern chain in sight.
storefronts: corner drug store with a hand-painted 'REXALL DRUGS' style sign and a small neon 'PRESCRIPTIONS' sign in the window
'BEN FRANKLIN' five & dime with red-and-white lettering
local diner with a hand-lettered 'HOT COFFEE 25¢' sign in the window
barber shop with a spinning red-white-and-blue barber pole by the door
hardware store with 'PAINT — GLASS — KEYS' painted on the display window
shoe repair shop with a small 'SHOE REPAIR — WHILE U WAIT' sign
appliance store window displaying console televisions with a 'COLOR TV' placard
women's dress shop with gold-leaf window lettering
people_activities: a woman in a midi skirt window-shopping at the five & dime
two men in wide-lapel jackets talking outside the diner
a shopkeeper in an apron sweeping the sidewalk
a mother holding a child's hand walking past the drug store
an older man reading a folded newspaper on a bench
a delivery man carrying stacked boxes into a storefront
a teenager leaning on a bicycle near the curb
a barber visible through the shop window cutting hair
a couple leaving the diner, the man holding the door
a mail carrier with a leather bag making rounds
extras: 2-3 bicycles leaned against a storefront wall
a glass-and-aluminum telephone booth on the corner
a painted ghost-sign wall advertisement on brick, weathered but still clearly readable
a wooden newspaper rack with stacked local papers by the diner door

gas_station.narrative: A full-service neighborhood gas station in 1975 — uniformed attendants pump the gas, check the oil, and wipe the windshield.
storefronts: tall roadside pole sign in the style of Phillips 66, Texaco, Conoco, Sinclair, or a generic independent brand
mechanical analog pumps with rotating numeral dials and glass-and-chrome detailing
porcelain-enamel brand signs on the building fascia
open service bay with a car raised on the lift inside
oil-can rack stacked with quart cans beside the pump island
chest-style soda vending machine by the office door
'FREE MAPS' and trading-stamp decals on the office window
tire display rack near the service bay
people_activities: a uniformed attendant actively fueling a vehicle at the pump
a second attendant wiping a customer's windshield
a mechanic in coveralls rolling a tire toward the service bay
a driver waiting behind the wheel while the attendant works
a customer paying the attendant cash through the car window
a mechanic leaning under the open hood of a car by the bay
a kid buying a bottle of soda from the machine
the station owner standing in the office doorway with a clipboard
extras: an air hose with a driveway service bell line across the apron
a stack of used tires beside the service bay
a water can and towel bucket on the pump island
a 'FREE AIR' sign next to the air pump

strip_mall.narrative: A brand-new mid-1970s strip mall — one low row of shops under a mansard-shingle canopy, fronted by an open parking lot.
storefronts: fabric and craft store with backlit plastic channel letters
small 'A&P' style grocery with stacked shopping carts by the door
laundromat with an 'OPEN 24 HOURS' sign and washers visible through the plate glass
family pharmacy with a backlit 'DRUGS — SUNDRIES' sign
record shop with 'HI-FI — STEREO — 8-TRACK TAPES' window lettering
soft-serve ice cream stand at the end cap with a cone-shaped sign
'TRUE VALUE' style hardware store with a red sign
beauty salon with 'PERMS — SETS — WIGS' hand-lettered on the glass
people_activities: a woman pushing a shopping cart across the parking lot
a man loading paper grocery bags into a car trunk
two teenagers sharing soft-serve cones by the end cap
a mother herding two kids toward the pharmacy
a shopper browsing the record store window
a workman in uniform carrying a toolbox to his truck
a woman carrying a laundry basket under the canopy
an elderly couple walking slowly along the covered walkway
extras: a coin-operated kiddie ride by a storefront door
a row of nested shopping carts near the grocery entrance
a telephone booth at the edge of the parking lot
painted white parking stall lines, slightly faded

default.narrative: An ordinary American commercial street in 1975 — modest local businesses, bold simple signage, wide quiet pavement.
storefronts: corner grocery with hand-painted produce prices on butcher paper in the window
drug store with a hand-painted sign and a neon 'PRESCRIPTIONS' sign in the window
local diner with a hand-lettered 'HOT COFFEE 25¢' sign
barber shop with a spinning barber pole
auto parts store with a red-and-white 'WESTERN AUTO' style sign
small bank branch with bronze letters and a round clock sign
five & dime with red 'BEN FRANKLIN' style lettering
people_activities: a shopkeeper in an apron sweeping the sidewalk
a woman in a midi skirt window-shopping
an older man reading a newspaper on a bench
kids riding bicycles along the street edge
a delivery man wheeling a hand truck of boxes
a couple walking slowly past the storefronts
a mail carrier making rounds with a leather bag
a man in a polyester suit checking his watch at the bus stop
extras: a glass telephone booth on the corner
a painted ghost-sign wall advertisement, weathered but readable
